what_it_does: Documents Option A, a read-only derived projection of the channel-memory noticeboard into a separate Obsidian vault, where the runtime owns the source of truth and Obsidian is only a reader.
observations: This item is a design option labeled P6 · Option A.
It proposes a regenerated mirror vault at vaults/company-lens/ with one markdown file per item, typed edges rendered as [[…]] companions, and .base files for saved views.
Obsidian would open the vault read-only and its writes would not be honored.
It lists gains: untouched truth, zero conflict, and free use of graph view, backlinks, search, Dataview, and Canvas.
It explicitly states the cost: it cannot accept any input, so it fails the goal of using the iOS Obsidian app as an annotation entry point.
It positions itself as the conservative baseline or floor, not the final answer.
standouts: The read-only lens design eliminates write conflicts by having only one writer.
It openly admits that it fails a stated user goal (mobile annotation through Obsidian).
It introduces .base files for saved views alongside per-item markdown files.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard frontmatter schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history.
Uses a Markdown heading and bullet list to present a design option.
Links to a parent item via a part_of relation to board://item-80c37615.
Uses bold text to highlight key terms and structured pros/cons.
notes: A design-option write-up arguing for a one-way, runtime-generated Obsidian lens as the safe baseline.
questions: What event triggers regeneration of the mirror vault and how frequently does it run?
How would the system behave if a user somehow edits the supposedly read-only vault?
What is the exact mapping from typed edges to the labels inside [[…]] companions?
What does a .base file contain and how is it consumed by Obsidian?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file states it is documenting Option A and does so, including both benefits and the explicit limitation.
intention_signals: Explore the safest possible Obsidian integration by treating the vault as a derived, read-only view of the canonical noticeboard, preserving all read features while eliminating write-conflict risk.
novelty: The explicit framing of a read-only projection as the conservative 'floor' that still delivers value even if the larger goal of bidirectional mobile editing is abandoned.
contribution: A clearly bounded integration pattern (one-way regenerated mirror) and an honest statement of its limitation, which a fused system can keep as a fallback or reference design.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard design option proposing a read-only, runtime-generated Obsidian vault projection of channel-memory items with wikilink companions and .base view files.
intention_for_embedding: To evaluate a conservative, one-way Obsidian lens that avoids write conflicts while acknowledging it cannot satisfy mobile annotation input.
